papers.title_en: Bridge-WA: Predicting Where and How the World Changes for Robotic Action
papers.authors: Yongjie Bai, Hanting Wang, Mingtong Dai, Qijun Zhong, Yang Liu, Liang Lin
papers.evidence: 世界-动作框架预测世界变化位置和方式以指导动作
papers.tldr: 通用视觉-语言-动作模型需要预测动作相关场景变化，但现有方法依赖大型生成模型或密集未来展开，开销大且关注弱相关细节。本文提出Bridge-WA，轻量级世界-动作框架，蒸馏冻结的未来变化教师模型为三个紧凑先验（未来token、变化图、运动流图），并通过WorldBridge条件化动作transformer，推理时移除教师。在VLABench、RoboTwin2.0、LIBERO-Plus及真实机器人上，Bridge-WA提升任务成功率、进度和鲁棒性，尤其在分布外视觉变化下。通过聚焦场景变化位置和方式，抑制背景、光照等无关因素，无需部署时密集未来生成，实现更好泛化。
papers.abstract_en: General-purpose vision-language-action models benefit from large vision-language priors, but effective manipulation also requires anticipating action-relevant scene changes. Existing world-action models often rely on large generative world models or dense future rollouts, which are expensive and spend capacity on visual details weakly coupled to control. We present Bridge-WA, a lightweight world-action framework that distills a frozen future-change teacher into three compact priors: future tokens for intended outcomes, change maps for intervention support, and motion-flow maps for local transition direction. A WorldBridge conditions the action transformer on these priors through multi-source attention memories and spatial-temporal biases, while the teacher model is removed at inference. Across VLABench, RoboTwin2.0, LIBERO-Plus and real-robot evaluations, Bridge-WA improves task success, progress, and robustness, with particularly clear gains under out-of-distribution visual shifts. By focusing action generation on where and how the scene will change, Bridge-WA suppresses nuisance appearance factors such as background, lighting, and distractors, leading to better generalization without deployment-time dense future-image generation. Code and visualizations are available at: https://hcplab-sysu.github.io/BRIDGE-WA .

papers.title_en: Hierarchical 3D Scene Graph Construction and Belief-based Planning for Semantic Navigation
papers.authors: Bing Wu, Zuyao Chen, Changwen Chen
papers.evidence: 通过层次化3D场景图作为内部环境模型进行规划
papers.tldr: 针对语义导航中缺乏全局结构化表示导致探索低效和短视行为的问题，提出零样本导航框架。该方法增量构建在线分层3D场景图（HSG）作为多粒度语义拓扑，并引入分层信念规划，融合语义先验与探索证据进行有限时间推演以评估宏动作长期回报。在多种高保真仿真任务中，尤其在长距离场景中，成功率提升9.4%，SPL提升5.0%，验证了全局规划的有效性。
papers.abstract_en: Semantic navigation is a fundamental task for embodied agents operating in unseen environments, requiring both semantic understanding and long-term decision-making. Recent foundation models have empowered agents with rich semantic priors for this task. However, without structured global representations, decision-making often falls back on local observations and greedy strategies, resulting in inefficient exploration and myopic behaviors, especially in long-distance navigation. To address these challenges, we propose a zero-shot semantic navigation framework. Our method incrementally maintains an online Hierarchical 3D Scene Graph (HSG) to form a multi-granular semantic topology over objects, zones, and regions, serving as a compact state abstraction for global planning. Building on this memory, we introduce a hierarchical belief-based planning framework that fuses semantic priors with exploration evidence on the HSG, and performs finite-horizon rollouts on an HSG-based simulator to explicitly estimate the long-term expected returns of candidate macro-actions. This enables globally consistent decisions and reduces redundant backtracking. Extensive experiments in high-fidelity simulation environments across multiple tasks and datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, particularly in long-distance scenarios, where our approach improves SR and SPL by an average of 9.4\% and 5.0\%, respectively.

papers.title_en: BIFROST: Bridging Invariant Feature Representation for Observation-space Sim2Real Transfer
papers.evidence: 从观测中学习不变特征用于机器人策略的仿真到现实迁移
papers.tldr: 机器人策略学习的sim2real迁移面临视觉和动力学差距。现有方法常分别处理各差距，但忽视了仿真与现实任务间的共享结构。BIFROST通过跨域双模拟目标学习共享历史编码器，将产生等价长期行为的观测-动作序列映射到相近潜在状态，在视觉导航和接触式操作任务中实现零样本迁移，性能优于领域自适应和协同训练基线，首次从原始观测中有效利用共享结构。
papers.abstract_en: Sim2real transfer for robot policy learning suffers due to mismatch between simulation and reality. Existing methods typically address each gap in isolation through separate adaptation modules, which are composed or layered when both gaps coexist. Yet the basis for attempting sim2real in the first place is that there is shared structure between a task in simulation and reality, where equivalent actions from equivalent configurations produce equivalent long term outcomes regardless of domain specific differences in rendering or physics. In this paper, we study whether we can identify and exploit this shared structure from raw observations to train a policy that enables zero shot transfer. We introduce BIFROST, which learns a shared history encoder on paired cross-domain data via cross-domain bisimulation objective: observation-action sequences leading to equivalent long-term behavior are mapped to nearby latent states, regardless of domain. Policies trained on these latent states in simulation transfer zero-shot to reality. We provide empirical evidence on sim2sim visual navigation and sim2real contact rich manipulation task and visual servoing task that BIFROST achieves effective transfer where domain adaptation and co-training baselines fail under both visual and dynamics domain gaps.

papers.title_en: Rank-Then-Act: Reward-Free Control from Frame-Order Progress
papers.authors: Yuriy Maksyuta, George Bredis, Ruslan Rakhimov, Daniil Gavrilov
papers.evidence: 从专家视频演示学习控制策略，无需奖励
papers.tldr: 从专家视频演示中学习控制策略通常需要设计奖励函数。本文提出Rank-Then-Act (RTA)框架，无需环境奖励，通过训练视觉语言模型对打乱帧序列进行排序得到进度评分，并利用Spearman秩相关系数作为奖励信号进行强化学习。在离散和连续控制任务上，RTA匹配或超越先前方法，且单个评分器可跨任务复用。该方法为无奖励策略学习提供了可扩展的替代方案。
papers.abstract_en: We introduce Rank-Then-Act (RTA), a framework for learning control policies from expert video demonstrations without environment rewards. RTA trains a Vision-Language Model (VLM) offline as a progress-based ordinal scorer, using a Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) objective over shuffled frame sequences, which forces the model to recover temporal ordering from visual semantics rather than trivial time cues. Importantly, instead of using the scorer directly as a scalar reward model, we propose a correlation-based reward function for reinforcement learning: at each interaction window, we compute the Spearman rank correlation between predicted progress rankings and true temporal indices, yielding a bounded, scale-invariant learning signal. This design decouples reward learning from absolute calibration and enables stable transfer across tasks and environments. We evaluate RTA on discrete control benchmarks (PyBoy: Catrap, Kirby) and continuous control tasks (PointMaze, MetaWorld). RTA consistently matches or outperforms prior video-based reward learning methods and rank-based baselines, while demonstrating strong cross-task reuse of a single pretrained progress scorer. Our results suggest that correlation-structured supervision over video-derived ordinal signals is sufficient for policy learning, offering a scalable alternative to explicit reward design.

papers.title_en: STEAM: Self-Supervised Temporal Ensemble Advantage Modeling for Real-World Robot Learning
papers.authors: Zhihao Liu, Qiuyi Gu, Yitao Wang, Dongming Qiao, Yixian Zhang, Shuaihang Chen, Liangzhi Shi, Tianxing Zhou, Zefang Huang, Kang Chen, Zhen Guo, Quanlu Zhang, Jincheng Yu, Xiaodan Liang, Guoliang Fan, Yu Wang, Feng Gao, Xinlei Chen, Chao Yu
papers.evidence: 从专家演示中自监督学习帧级优势以辅助机器人策略学习
papers.tldr: 真实世界机器人学习常面临异质数据，其中夹杂停滞、修正等次优行为。STEAM提出一种无标签的自监督方法，通过训练集成时间偏移预测器从专家演示中学习帧级优势，并取最小优势保守评分混合质量数据。在双人毛巾折叠、收银台、补货和单臂拾放任务中，STEAM识别了停滞、失败和恢复，结合CFGRL将成功率分别提升59%、54.3%、23%和16.2%。该方法无需人工标注，为策略学习提供可靠局部信号。
papers.abstract_en: Real-world robot learning increasingly relies on heterogeneous data, but demonstrations and rollouts often mix useful progress with stalls, corrections, and suboptimal behavior. Effective policy learning therefore requires frame-level advantages that distinguish reliable local progress from failures and regressions. We propose Self-supervised Temporal Ensemble Advantage Modeling (STEAM), a label-free method that learns such advantages from expert demonstrations. STEAM trains an ensemble of temporal-offset predictors on frame pairs within expert trajectories, using the normalized temporal offset between two frames as a self-supervised signal. Each predictor maps a frame pair to a distribution over temporal offsets, which is converted into a scalar advantage. STEAM then takes the minimum advantage across the ensemble to score mixed-quality rollout data conservatively. Across real-world bimanual towel folding, chip checkout, cola restocking, and single-arm pick-and-place tasks, STEAM identifies stalls, failures, and recoveries. When combined with CFGRL, STEAM further improves policy success rate by 59%, 54.3%, 23% and 16.2% over baselines, respectively.

papers.title_en: Grasp-Oriented Non-Prehensile Manipulation via Learning a Graspability Field
papers.evidence: 通过强化学习和可抓取性场进行机器人抓取操作
papers.tldr: 针对机器人抓取前的非抓取操作需要预定义目标姿态的局限，本文提出以物体抓取能力场为目标优化，通过强化学习学习闭环操作策略，无需外部规划器和手动终止条件。该方法定义抓取能力场衡量物体配置的抓取可行性，提供密集学习信号指导策略将物体重新配置至可抓取状态并自动过渡到抓取。实验证明策略有效提升抓取成功率，且预测的抓取能力距离与真实成功相关。
papers.abstract_en: Non-prehensile manipulation is often used as a preparatory step for robotic grasping, yet existing approaches typically require a predefined target object pose. In practice, however, objects admit multiple graspable configurations and the desired pose is not known in advance. We reformulate non-prehensile manipulation for grasping as optimizing an object centric graspability objective rather than reaching a specific pose. We construct a graspable set from synthesized grasps and define a graspability field that measures how suitable an object configuration is for successful grasp execution. The scalar measure provides a dense learning signal for reinforcement learning and determines when to terminate manipulation. This yields a closed-loop manipulation-to-grasp pipeline driven by a single policy. Experiments in simulation and on a real robot show that the policy reliably reconfigures objects into graspable states and transitions to grasping without external planners or manually specified stopping conditions. The predicted graspability distance correlates with real world grasp success, which indicates that the learned representation captures grasp feasibility of object configurations.

papers.title_en: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
papers.authors: Marcel Torne, Anubha Mahajan, Abhijnya Bhat, Chelsea Finn
papers.evidence: 基于自由形式人类偏好的机器人操作学习，通过人类反馈与模仿学习相关
papers.tldr: 机器人长程操作任务中，稀疏的成功标签和模糊的二元偏好难以提供充分的学习信号。本文提出Freeform Preference Learning (FPL)，让标注员用自然语言定义偏好轴（如速度、安全）并提供沿轴的成对比较，学习语言条件奖励模型，训练奖励条件策略。在多个真实和模拟任务中，FPL相比稀疏奖励和二元偏好方法平均提升38%，并学到密集进度信号。该方法还支持用户测试时通过语言调整策略行为，展现组合性而无需重训练。
papers.abstract_en: Reward design remains a central bottleneck for autonomous robot policy improvement, especially in long-horizon manipulation tasks where sparse success labels provide too little signal and binary preferences collapse many competing notions of quality into one ambiguous signal. We introduce Freeform Preference Learning (FPL), a method for learning robot policies from freeform human preferences. Rather than asking annotators which of two trajectories is better overall, FPL lets them define natural-language preference axes, such as speed, safety, quality of placement, or carefulness, and provide pairwise preferences along each axis. These annotations are used to learn a language-conditioned reward model that maps a trajectory and preference label to an axis-specific reward. We use this model to train a reward-conditioned policy that optimizes across the multiple human-specified dimensions. Across four real-world and two simulated long-horizon manipulation tasks, FPL improves over sparse-reward and binary-preference methods by 38 percentage points. Beyond improved performance, FPL learns dense progress signals without explicit subtask segmentation, shows compositionality of behavior not present in the data, and allows users to steer the policy towards different behaviors at test time without retraining. Blog post with videos available at https://freeform-pl.github.io/fpl.website/
